title: Vitellogenin Genes: Master Regulators of Insect Fertility and Emerging Targets for Biomedical Control
description: A synthesis of current research on the critical role of vitellogenin (Vg) genes and their receptors (VgR) in insect female reproduction. The article explores molecular mechanisms of Vg synthesis and uptake, hormonal regulation, and the use of RNA interference (RNAi) for functional analysis and pest control. It also addresses the impact of nutritional and environmental stress on fertility and validates the Vg/VgR system as a target for innovative insect population control strategies through cross-species comparative analyses.
